pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE, Jan. 22-In the rail-
road hearing before Interstate Com
merce Commissioner Lane, the fact
Is established that competition be-
tween the Southern Pacific and
Union Pacific systems on coast
shipments ceased with merger ef-
fected by Hartman. Prior to that
time the two roads had been max
ing competitive rates to Portland.


This evidence was given by Ben
jamin Campbell, now traffic mana
ger of the Great Northern and at
the time Of the merger general
manager of the o, r. q NJ, part
of the Hartman combine.


E. D. Farrell testified to his con-
nection with the Oregon & Wash
ington railroad, another Hartman
feeder, but did not know where the
money needed to operate it came
from. He reported direct to Harri.
man, however.


Farrell also testified that Harri-
man was a director of the Pacific
Coast Co., operating coast steam,
ships, but did not know how much
stock he controlled.


Minor officials of the Hartman
roads testified that there was no
effort made to secure business in
competition between the roads in.
cluded in the combine.
